description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly reddish-brown coloration. The surface is marked by a mix of rough, jagged edges and flatter planes, with visible patches of lighter tan and white mineral deposits scattered across its exterior. The stone exhibits natural weathering, with some areas appearing more eroded and pitted, while others retain sharper, freshly fractured lines. Its texture is coarse and gritty, suggesting a high degree of surface roughness typical of sedimentary or weathered igneous rock. The coloration varies subtly across its faces, with deeper rust tones on one side and lighter, more mottled areas on others. This stone would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features, serving as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A rugged, reddish-brown stone with jagged edges, rough texture, and patches of lighter mineral deposits. Its surface shows signs of natural weathering and color variation.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and flat with a mix of reddish-brown and tan hues, showing a rough, uneven surface with visible mineral patches.
description_view_1: This view highlights the stone's angular, wedge-like profile and reveals more of its lighter, mottled surface areas.
description_view_2: The stone looks darker and more uniform in color, with a prominent, relatively flat face and fewer visible mineral inclusions.
description_view_3: A pointed, triangular aspect is visible, with pronounced layering and a mix of rough and smoother surfaces.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or garden path
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and veining with scattered mineral patches; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible signs of natural erosion and minor surface chipping.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or vegetation when used in landscaping.
